<!-- auto-generated by build_wiki.py -->
# st-fact — Fact-check stories in a container

Sends a story to an AI and asks it to fact-check every claim, scoring each one True, Partially True, Opinion, Partially False, or False. Appends the result to the container's `fact[]` array.

Use `--ai-review` to get an AI-generated digest of **existing** fact-check results — no new checks are run. Useful for a quick summary of what multiple checkers found false without reading every raw report.

**Run after:** `st-prep`  ·  **Run before:** `st-fix` · `st-heatmap` · `st-verdict`

---

## Examples

```bash
# Run fact-check
st-fact subject.json                     # fact-check story 1 with default AI
st-fact -s 2 --ai gemini subject.json   # fact-check story 2 with Gemini
st-fact --ai all subject.json           # run all 5 AIs in parallel
st-fact --no-cache subject.json         # bypass API cache

# Review existing results (no new API calls for fact-checking)
st-fact --ai-review subject.json        # AI digest: accuracy %, false/~false findings
st-fact --ai-review --ai-caption subject.json  # short version (≤80 words)
st-fact --ai-review -s 2 subject.json   # review story 2
st-fact --ai-review --ai openai subject.json   # use openai for the digest
```

---

## Options

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `-s N` / `--story N` | Select story N (default: 1) |
| `--ai <name>` | AI provider for fact-checking or review (default: configured default) |
| `--ai all` | Run all AI providers in parallel (fact-check only) |
| `--ai-review` | Generate AI digest of existing fact-check results; no new checks run |
| `--ai-caption` | With `--ai-review`: short caption (≤80 words) instead of full digest |
| `--no-cache` | Bypass API cache |
| `--all` | Fact-check every story in the container |
| `-v` / `-q` | Verbose / quiet output |

---

## `--ai-review` Output

The digest covers three sections:

1. **Accuracy verdict** — overall % of claims True/~True vs False/~False; average score across all checkers
2. **Key problem claims** — summarises every False/Partially False/~True finding; groups repeated findings across multiple checkers; quotes or paraphrases the specific claim and what was wrong
3. **Bottom line** — one-sentence verdict on the article's overall factual accuracy

Use `--ai-caption` for a single-paragraph ≤80-word version.

Fact entries with missing or `null` counts (e.g. a checker that timed out) are automatically skipped.

---

## Related

[st-cross](st-cross.md) · [st-fix](st-fix.md) · [st-verdict](st-verdict.md) · [st-heatmap](st-heatmap.md)

---

## For Developers

Splits the story into segments via `mmd_util.build_segments()`, sends each segment to the AI, and appends a `fact[]` entry. The entry includes `score`, `counts[5]`, `summary`, `report`, `claims[]` (per-segment structured verdicts with `seg_id`, `verdict`, `explanation`), and `timing{}`.

`--ai-review` reads the existing `fact[]` entries, aggregates counts and collects all False/~False/~True claims from every checker's `claims[]`, and passes the digest to the AI. Fact entries where `counts` is `None` or has fewer than 5 elements are skipped silently.
